comments.body:
Closing this. The reported change is a narrow docs/version-marker drift from an external audit, not a maintainer-prioritized product bug, and this author does not pass the contribution gate. We can revisit it later if it becomes part of a maintainer-owned docs sync pass.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. Adding runnable-agent frontmatter to documentation templates just to satisfy an external scan is not a product fix for oh-my-claudecode, and it muddies the contract for files that are intentionally templates, not installed agents.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. Adding runnable-agent frontmatter to documentation templates just to satisfy an external scan is not a product fix for oh-my-claudecode, and it muddies the contract for files that are intentionally templates, not installed agents.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. This is an external audit-driven policy change, not a confirmed product bug in oh-my-claudecode. The current skill intentionally uses explicit commands for MCP setup; adding extra confirmation friction based on a third-party audit is not a maintainer-accepted fix path here.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. This is an external audit-driven workflow/policy change, not a narrowly reproduced product bug. We are not taking a broad behavior change to `omc-doctor` from a no-star audit sweep PR. If maintainers decide to redesign overwrite/diff UX later, that should come from a maintainer-owned track.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Implemented in 512fa22 with a more compact format to match the post-v9.1.0 style.

The core idea (10-step sequential workflow with dependency ordering) is preserved. Thank you for the finding — the orphan-detection dependency on inbound-link indexing was a real gap.
Fixed in v9.1.0 — all versions unified to 9.1.0 in commit 88cf546. Thank you for catching this!

Closing as superseded by #333, which covers both web-auth and web-browse hardcoded-path fixes in a single PR. The fix in this PR is a strict subset of #333.

Same NLPM finding surfaced in re-audit issue #341; will close #341 once #333 and #339 merge.

Thanks for the audit!

Closing as superseded by #333, which covers both web-auth and web-browse hardcoded-path fixes in a single PR. The fix in this PR is a strict subset of #333.

Same NLPM finding surfaced in re-audit issue #341; will close #341 once #333 and #339 merge.

Thanks for the audit!

Closing as duplicate of #335. Note: the underlying proposal is also being declined - Avi commented on #335 that caret ranges are intentional here so runtime security patches flow in automatically; the lockfile already pins exact versions and surfaces actual updates in PRs.

Thanks for the audit nonetheless!

reason: This is a manually-invoked developer utility for displaying ASCII art; it has no automated call surface and is not reachable by external input in normal usage.
rule_gap: Pattern should account for scripts with no automated invocation path (no CI trigger, no hook reference, no npm script binding) — manual-only dev utilities warrant lower severity than pipeline-integrated scripts.

reason: The command contains no tool invocations; it only emits a text message telling the user to switch skills. allowed-tools is irrelevant for a pure-text redirect stub.
rule_gap: The missing-allowed-tools rule should be suppressed or reduced for commands whose body contains no tool calls.

reason: The command contains no tool invocations; it only emits a text message telling the user to switch skills. allowed-tools is irrelevant for a pure-text redirect stub.
rule_gap: The missing-allowed-tools rule should be suppressed or reduced for commands whose body contains no tool calls.

reason: The command contains no tool invocations; it only emits a text message telling the user to switch skills. allowed-tools is irrelevant for a pure-text redirect stub.
rule_gap: The missing-allowed-tools rule should be suppressed or reduced for commands whose body contains no tool calls.

reason: A deprecation stub whose only behavior is to emit a fixed redirect message requires no input-branch logic — there is nothing to handle.
rule_gap: The no-empty-input-handling rule should not penalise commands whose body is a single unconditional message.

reason: Single unconditional redirect message; empty input handling inapplicable.
rule_gap: The no-empty-input-handling rule should not penalise commands whose body is a single unconditional message.

reason: Single unconditional redirect message; empty input handling inapplicable.
rule_gap: The no-empty-input-handling rule should not penalise commands whose body is a single unconditional message.

reason: The curl|sh string is the argument to `echo`, not a shell command; it will never be executed by the script.
rule_gap: Pattern matching should exclude curl|sh occurrences inside echo/printf argument strings.

reason: base64 is used as a transport mechanism to pass a local Python string to a remote E2B sandbox over stdin; the content is constructed in the same file with no external input.
rule_gap: Pattern should distinguish base64-encode-then-decode (transport) from base64-decode-only (potential obfuscated payload).

comments.body:
Thanks for catching this @xiaolai — single quotes blocking expansion is a real bug.

Closing in favor of 1e07cba, which makes one additional change: the variable name itself was wrong. Per the [Claude Code hooks docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks), the injected variable is ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, not ${PLUGIN_DIR} (which Claude Code doesn't inject — it would have expanded to an empty string after this PR's quote fix). The rest of the codebase already uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} consistently in skills/understand/SKILL.md etc., so this aligns the hook with that.

Credited you as co-author on the commit. Appreciate the audit!

reason: This is an intentional test fixture designed to verify the agnix dangerous-command detection rule fires; the file is under tests/fixtures/invalid/ and is never executed.
rule_gap: Scanner should skip files under tests/fixtures/ or files with 'invalid' in their path prefix when checking for dangerous patterns, or suppress findings when the file type is a test fixture for a linter.

comments.body: those are not agent commands, just skill reference files. so no frontmatter needed

reason: curl targets localhost only; no external network exposure or data exfiltration possible
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between localhost health checks and external network calls to avoid noise

reason: curl targets localhost only; no external network exposure or data exfiltration possible
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between localhost health checks and external network calls to avoid noise

reason: The pattern appears exclusively inside a `cat <<'USAGE'` heredoc that is printed to stdout as documentation text; it is never executed by the script
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

reason: Same as line 57: inert documentation text inside a heredoc, never executed
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

reason: Same as lines 57-58: inert documentation text inside a heredoc, never executed
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

reason: Inert documentation text in heredoc, never executed — identical to the false positive in scripts/install.sh
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should detect whether the match is inside an executed command or a string literal

reason: Nano Banana Pro is a real Google product/codename for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview image-generation model, not unreplaced placeholder text
rule_gap: BUG-placeholder-text rule should not flag marketing/product names paired with backticked model IDs as placeholders; needs an allowlist of known Google model codenames or a heuristic that recognizes "<codename> for <backticked-model-id>" as a valid pairing

reason: Nano Banana is a real Google product/codename for the gemini-2.5-flash-image image-generation model, not unreplaced placeholder text
rule_gap: BUG-placeholder-text rule should not flag marketing/product names paired with backticked model IDs as placeholders; needs an allowlist of known Google model codenames or a heuristic that recognizes "<codename> for <backticked-model-id>" as a valid pairing

comments.body: Thanks for the fix. We are replacing this with #172, which applies the same least-privilege direction but removes `Bash` as well because the status command only needs to read and glob spec files.
Closing in favor of #172.
Thanks for the fix. We are replacing this with #172 so the Gemini agent frontmatter uses Gemini CLI-compatible tool names and YAML array syntax.
Closing in favor of #172.

reason: The same sentence immediately provides the concrete minimum (44x44px), making 'appropriately sized' a stylistic lead-in rather than an undefined vague claim
rule_gap: Rule R09 should allow vague qualifier immediately followed by a concrete measurement on the same line

reason: subprocess.run with a list and no shell=True cannot be exploited via shell metacharacter injection regardless of string content
rule_gap: SEC-shell-true should distinguish list-form subprocess.run (safe) from string-form or shell=True invocations

comments.body: This is official GitHub SpecKit files. No need to modify them.
Incorporated into v2.0.0 (commit 6e8a28a). `name: docs-manager` added to `.opencode/agents/docs-manager.md`. Thank you @xiaolai — your NLPM audit directly shaped this release.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. All 11 OpenCode commands now have explicit `name` fields. Thank you @xiaolai.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. `allowed-tools` added to all 19 Claude Code + claude-plugin command files. Thank you @xiaolai.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. Both sync scripts now pass \$DST as `sys.argv[1]` instead of string interpolation. Valid security fix — thank you @xiaolai.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. `sync_dir()` now validates destination path depth before `rm -rf`. Thank you @xiaolai.

Thanks for the audit, @xiaolai! Closing this one — going to keep parity with upstream.

**Reason**: Per the [official Claude Code slash-commands frontmatter spec](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands.md#frontmatter-reference), the `name` field is **not required** — it's derived from the filename when omitted. Upstream `obra/superpowers` ships these same three deprecated stubs without `name`, so adding it here would create unnecessary divergence and friction when re-syncing translations. The three files are also slated for removal in the next major release.

NLPM 这条规则属误报；保留与上游一致的形态更利于后续翻译同步。三个 stub 本就是下个 major 要删的过渡文件，不影响功能。

---

感谢 @xiaolai 的自动化审计！这条作为误报关闭，理由：Claude Code 官方规范中命令的 `name` 字段非必需（缺省时从文件名推导），上游同款 3 个 deprecated stub 也未带 `name`，加上反而会让后续翻译同步多出无意义冲突。

Thanks @xiaolai — adopted the spirit of this PR but went straight to `>=20.0.0` instead of `>=18.0.0`.

**Why 20, not 18**: As of today (2026-04-28), Node 18 also reached end-of-life in April 2025. Bumping to 18 would land us on another EOL line; 20 is the current oldest still-maintained LTS (22/24 are active LTS / current). Going to 20 avoids a redundant bump in a few weeks.

Landed in main as 81f746a.

---

感谢 @xiaolai 的方向是对的，但落地为 `>=20.0.0` 而非 `>=18.0.0`：今天是 2026-04-28，Node 18 已于 2025-04 EOL，bump 到 18 等于又落到一条 EOL 线上。20 是当下仍在维护的最低 LTS（22/24 为 active），一次到位省一轮。已合入 main：81f746a。

Thanks for the PR. Since this was opened, #1188 removed the deprecated legacy slash commands on `dev`, including all three command files this PR updates. With those command stubs gone, there is no frontmatter left to fix.

Closing this as obsolete.

Thanks for your pull request! It looks like this may be your first contribution to a Google open source project. Before we can look at your pull request, you'll need to sign a Contributor License Agreement (CLA).

@googlebot I signed it!
No longer needed.

reason: Claude Code execution context already has user-level filesystem access; writing to /tmp and executing a self-constructed script is not a privilege escalation vector in this threat model.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish self-constructed ephemeral scripts from scripts whose content derives from external or user-controlled input.

reason: grep runs inside the user's own checked-out repository with no cross-user boundary; worst case is the user auditing a directory they chose themselves.
rule_gap: Rule should account for the absence of a privilege boundary when the tool runs as the user who invoked it.

> [!WARNING]
> You have reached your daily quota limit. Please wait up to 24 hours and I will start processing your requests again!

LGTM
Thanks!

Thanks for the audit - the issue you flagged is real. Closing in favor of the consolidated structural fix in #840, which addresses the root cause:

- Extends `scripts/sync-rule-bookkeeping.js` `COUNT_FILES` to cover `knowledge-base/README.md`, `knowledge-base/INDEX.md`, `plugin/commands/agnix.md`, `plugin/skills/agnix/SKILL.md`, `skills/agnix/SKILL.md`, and `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` so the rule-count drift you caught cannot recur.
- Syncs `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` + `npm/package.json` `version` fields from `Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version` so the version drift you caught also cannot recur.
- Wires `--check` into both `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (already there) and `scripts/pre-push-rust` (new), so both remote and local gates refuse to ship a drifted tree.
- One-time sweep applied all current drift (405 → 415 rules, 0.16.1 → 0.23.0 plugin, 0.20.0 → 0.23.0 npm, + the `allowed-tools` fix from #830).

If #840 merges without applying one of your findings correctly, please reopen. Sorry for the delay in getting back to the automated audits.

Sorry for your token waste, but this is intentional

reason: All three interpolated values originate from trusted internal sources (Sparkle sign_update binary, stat command, mktemp) and cannot contain Python-injectable characters.
rule_gap: The eval-with-variables rule should account for cases where the interpolated values are constrained to safe character sets (base64, integers, filesystem paths from mktemp).

reason: subprocess.run with a list argument and implicit shell=False has no command-injection surface; the pre-scan rule matched on subprocess.run presence without inspecting the argument type.
rule_gap: Rule should require shell=True to be present and True, not just subprocess.run presence, to reduce false positives on safe list-form invocations.

reason: Content only reads process.version and process.platform with no system modification or network access; purely read-only environment validation
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish preinstall scripts that are verifiably read-only environment checks from those that modify system state

## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request addresses a functional bug in the recipe-post-mortem-setup skill where incorrect CLI flags were causing the workflow to fail. By restructuring the document creation process and updating the command syntax, the post-mortem setup is now correctly aligned with the expected gws CLI behavior. ### Highlights * **Corrected CLI flags**: Updated the gws docs +write command to use the correct --document and --text flags instead of the invalid --title and --body flags. * **Workflow adjustment**: Split the document creation and content writing into two distinct steps to ensure a valid documentId is available before writing content.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request resolves a functional error in the 'recipe-collect-form-responses' skill where an invalid API call was being used to list forms. By switching to the Drive API, the recipe now correctly enables users to discover their form IDs, ensuring the subsequent steps in the workflow can execute successfully. ### Highlights * **API Correction**: Replaced the non-existent 'gws forms forms list' command with a valid 'gws drive files list' query to correctly identify Google Forms. * **Dependency Update**: Added 'gws-drive' as a required skill in the recipe metadata and documentation to support the new listing method.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. ## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request improves the reliability and security of the development environment by pinning dependencies during the installation of the cargo-llvm-cov tool. By enforcing the use of the lockfile, the installation process becomes deterministic, ensuring that every environment uses the exact same dependency graph intended by the tool maintainers. ### Highlights * **Reproducible Builds**: Added the --locked flag to the cargo install command for cargo-llvm-cov to ensure dependency versions are strictly respected. * **Security and Stability**: Prevented potential issues caused by silently pulling in new, untested, or incompatible transitive dependency versions during installation.

quote: No longer needed.
With those command stubs gone, there is no frontmatter left to fix.

reason: Script only invokes 'pnpm build' for internal workspace packages with no network calls, credential access, or dynamic code execution.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between postinstall scripts that perform internal deterministic builds vs those that fetch remote code or run arbitrary shell commands.

reason: husky is a well-known, widely-audited git hooks manager and 'prepare: husky' is its canonical recommended installation; no custom code is executed beyond husky's own init.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between known-safe tool invocations (husky, lint-staged) and custom scripts in prepare/postinstall hooks.

reason: '--shell=bun' selects Bun's cross-platform shell, which is more restricted than /bin/sh and does not enable arbitrary shell injection; this is a safer choice, not a risk.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between OS shell (sh/bash) and sandboxed runtime shells (bun shell, deno shell) which have narrower attack surfaces.

reason: shell:true is intentional design for a bridge script that must execute a user-configured downstream command; the script is not invoked by any hook in hooks.json and the command source is the developer, not untrusted hook input flowing from Claude's tool pipeline.
rule_gap: SEC-shell-true should distinguish between hook-invoked scripts processing user input and standalone dev-utility bridge scripts where shell:true is an expected implementation choice.

reason: tempfile.gettempdir() is the standard cross-platform mechanism for temporary directories; this is intentional test-isolation, not a security risk.
rule_gap: The rule should distinguish between intentional temp-dir isolation patterns and uncontrolled writes to /tmp with user-controlled paths.

reason: EVO_TRACES_DIR and EVO_RESULT_PATH are the documented evo SDK contract; they are always set by the trusted evo CLI, not by untrusted user input.
rule_gap: The rule should account for env vars that are part of a framework's published API contract, not arbitrary external input.

reason: PostHog client project keys are intentionally public and must be embedded in browser code per PostHog's architecture; this is not a secret key.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between public client-side analytics keys (PostHog, Segment write keys) and server-side secret keys to avoid false positives on standard analytics integrations.

reason: PostHog project API keys are intentionally public — they are used client-side to send analytics events and carry no write or admin privileges.
rule_gap: Rule should exempt client-side analytics keys (PostHog, Segment, Mixpanel) that are public by design from the hardcoded-credentials pattern.

reason: Client-side analytics identifiers (Reo, PostHog, GA) are intentionally embedded in public JS and carry no privileged access.
rule_gap: Rule should exempt analytics client IDs used in public browser scripts from hardcoded-credential findings.

reason: Script is built entirely from hardcoded template literals; API_CONFIG_MODULE is a path.resolve() result escaped with JSON.stringify(); no user input reaches the eval'd string; this is a test-harness isolation pattern
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish test-harness eval patterns (controlled inputs, isolated subprocess) from production code that passes external/user input to eval

reason: This is a standard IE compatibility shim inside the vendored React DOM production minified library; the repo author did not write this code and cannot be injected by application logic
rule_gap: Pattern scanner should exclude files in known vendor/library paths (src/main/resources/libs/) and check for MSApp context which indicates IE browser-API usage rather than arbitrary code execution

comments.body:
Thanks for the report. I reviewed this assuming it was invalid, and I do not think this is a valid security concern as stated.

`ORCA_ELECTRON_VITE_CLI` is an internal test hook on the local development runner. The threat model in the PR is "an attacker can control environment variables for `pnpm dev`"; at that point the attacker already has equivalent or stronger code-execution paths in a Node/pnpm dev process environment. Also, the proposed mitigation does not close the stated issue: the same attacker-controlled environment can set `NODE_ENV=test` along with `ORCA_ELECTRON_VITE_CLI` and still exercise the override.

So this should not land as a security fix. If we want to clean this up separately, the right direction would be to remove the env-based test hook entirely by refactoring the process-tree behavior into a testable module, not to treat `NODE_ENV` as a security boundary.

Validation performed:
- Reviewed the one-file diff in `config/scripts/run-electron-vite-dev.mjs`.
- Ran `pnpm exec vitest run --config config/vitest.config.ts src/main/startup/run-electron-vite-dev.test.ts`, which passes.
- A broader accidental `pnpm test -- ...` invocation ran the full suite and surfaced an unrelated existing failure in `src/main/codex-accounts/service.test.ts`.
Closing because this is not a valid security fix: env control for the dev runner already implies code execution, and the proposed NODE_ENV guard is bypassable from the same env-control position.

Thanks for the report. I reviewed this with the same bar as #974, and I do not think this is valid as a security fix.

The repo already commits `pnpm-lock.yaml`, and that lockfile pins the resolved versions for `@parcel/watcher`, `node-pty`, and `electron`. It also records the `node-pty@1.1.0` patch hash against the resolved `node-pty` package. A normal frozen install does not silently float these packages just because the manifest uses `^`; changing those versions requires an intentional lockfile refresh.

There is also a mechanical blocker in this PR: it changes `package.json` but does not update `pnpm-lock.yaml`. As a result, `pnpm install --lockfile-only --frozen-lockfile` fails with mismatched specifiers for all three dependencies:

- `@parcel/watcher`: lockfile `^2.5.6`, manifest `2.5.6`
- `node-pty`: lockfile `^1.1.0`, manifest `1.1.0`
- `electron`: lockfile `^41.2.2`, manifest `41.2.2`

Pinning native dependencies exactly can be a dependency-management policy choice, but this PR does not establish a concrete security vulnerability, and as submitted it would break CI/frozen installs.
Closing because this is not valid as a security fix, and the PR is mechanically broken: package.json specifiers changed without updating pnpm-lock.yaml, so frozen pnpm installs fail.
We don't need to do this for now. I will close this.

reason: Command string is completely hardcoded with no user-controlled interpolation; standard platform detection pattern with no injection surface
rule_gap: Rule should exempt execSync calls where the entire command string is a compile-time constant with no template interpolation

comments.body:
Thanks @xiaolai — great find, and we brought it in. One small change on our end: rather than hard-coding the limit at 2000 characters, we made it configurable via a `PM_MAX_INPUT` env var with a default of 4000. That way legitimate longer prompts can still run without touching the script:

```bash
PM_MAX_INPUT=8000 ./scripts/run-pm.sh skill prd-development "..."
```

The error message also tells you how to override it. Appreciate you looking out for the community — this is exactly the kind of contribution that makes the repo better for everyone.

reason: Writing to a user-configured output_dir is the plugin's explicit and documented purpose; the only write target is the shortcut output directory, not arbitrary paths
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish intentional plugin-output writes (declared in plugin manifest userConfig) from uncontrolled writes to arbitrary filesystem paths

reason: Codex does not use the same plugin frontmatter schema as Claude Code; the Codex skill body explicitly documents Codex-specific script invocation patterns
rule_gap: Rule should allow sibling-skill divergence when the skill explicitly documents its target platform's different invocation model
